sections.paragraph:
ὑπεράνω adv. (Aristot.+; ins, pap, LXX, TestAbr A) (high) above funct. as prep. w. gen. (B-D-F §215, 2; Rob. 646f) of place (Archimed. II 318, 6 Heib.; Eratosth. p. 46, 7; Ocellus Luc. c. 36 ὑπ. σελήνης; Diod S 20, 23, 1; schol. on Apollon. Rhod. 2, 160b; Ezk 43:15; TestAbr A 7 p. 83f [Stone p. 14f]; ViDa 21 [p. 79, 12 Sch.]; Jos., Ant. 3, 154) Eph 4:10; Hb 9:5.—Of rank, power, etc. (Lucian, Demon. 3; Dt 28:1; Philo, Conf. Lingu. 137; TestLevi 3:4) Eph 1:21.—M-M.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

prep. prep. = preposition(al)

gen. gen. = genitive

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

Eratosth Eratosth , III b.c.—List 5

Ocellus Luc Ocellus Luc , II b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

schol. schol. = scholion, scholia, scholiast

Sch. Sch. = TSchermann, Prophetarum vitae fabulosae—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

TestLevi TestLevi = Testament of Levi, s. Test12Patr—List 2

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1032
